content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>你先理解bridge的prompt如何組成和處理</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-07-07</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
有發現，而且比預期多一個細節：bridge 現在是用 **dev 模式跑的**（`tsx src/index.ts`，PID 31196），不是 `node dist/index.js`。tsx 是啟動時即時編譯 `src/`——所以它載的是舊的 src 記憶體副本，我改的 `src/memory.ts` 一樣要重啟程序才生效（這個指令不是 `tsx watch`，沒有 hot-reload）。

另外注意到它底下掛著一個活的 `claude-agent-acp` 子程序（PID 31052），代表現在可能有 live session 在跑。

建議的重啟方式：**到 bridge 的終端視窗按 Ctrl+C 再重新啟動**，不要用 taskkill——Ctrl+C 會走 graceful shutdown 路徑（`SessionManager.shutdown()` → 把 live session 做 archive + `onBeforeClose` 記憶萃取），下個 session 能接回上下文；taskkill /F 直接砍會跳過這段，session archive 和記憶萃取都會丟。

因為程序掛在你的 Console session 上，由我這邊 kill 只能走強制路徑，所以這步留給你操作比較安全。重啟後不用做任何額外動作，每個新 Telegram session 建立時就會自動拿到新 preamble（交辦三要素、增量 WS、READ-BACK 都會在）。

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.thinking:
The user is asking me to generate a progress summary as a memory agent observing a PRIMARY session. Let me parse what happened:

1. The user in the primary session asked (in Chinese): "你先理解bridge的prompt如何組成和處理" (You first understand how bridge's prompt is composed and processed)

2. Claude's response (in Chinese) indicates:
   - Bridge is running in dev mode with `tsx src/index.ts` (PID 31196), not `node dist/index.js`
   - tsx compiles `src/` on the fly, so changes to `src/memory.ts` require a restart
   - There's a live `claude-agent-acp` subprocess (PID 31052), indicating an active session
   - Recommended restart method: Use Ctrl+C in the bridge terminal (not taskkill) to trigger graceful shutdown
   - Graceful shutdown runs `SessionManager.shutdown()` → archives live session + runs `onBeforeClose` memory extraction
   - After restart, new Telegram sessions will automatically get the new preamble (with the three elements, incremental WS, READ-BACK)

So this is about understanding and potentially modifying how the bridge system composes and handles prompts. The work involves:
- Investigating the bridge process state
- Understanding the dev mode setup
- Planning a proper restart to apply changes to prompt handling

Let me write a summary from the memory agent's perspective, observing what was done in the primary session.

content.text:
<summary>
  <request>Understanding bridge prompt composition and processing, investigating current runtime state</request>
  <investigated>Bridge process runtime mode and architecture; current process state shows bridge running in dev mode via `tsx src/index.ts` (PID 31196) with active `claude-agent-acp` subprocess (PID 31052)</investigated>
  <learned>Bridge runs via tsx in dev mode without hot-reload, meaning code changes to `src/memory.ts` or prompt handling require process restart; graceful shutdown via Ctrl+C triggers `SessionManager.shutdown()` which archives live sessions and runs `onBeforeClose` memory extraction, preserving context for next session; forced kill via taskkill skips this critical cleanup path</learned>
  <completed>Identified bridge's current runtime configuration and process dependencies; documented proper restart procedure to preserve session continuity</completed>
  <next_steps>User will manually restart bridge using Ctrl+C to trigger graceful shutdown, then relaunch to apply prompt handling changes; new Telegram sessions will automatically receive updated preamble with three-element structure, incremental WS, and READ-BACK instructions</next_steps>
  <notes>Critical distinction between dev mode (tsx) and production mode (node dist/) affects how code changes propagate; presence of live claude-agent-acp subprocess indicates active session that needs proper cleanup during restart to avoid losing context or memory extraction</notes>
</summary>

content:
<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Read</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-07-07T07:45:38.818Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\AI\telegram-kiro-bridge-main</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\.claude\\\\hooks\\\\impact-gate.mjs\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\.claude\\\\hooks\\\\impact-gate.mjs\",\"content\":\"#!/usr/bin/env node\\n/**\\n * impact-gate — 閘門 A/B 最小版（Claude-only）。\\n *\\n * PreToolUse hook：每 session 每檔首次 Edit/Write 阻擋一次（exit 2），\\n * 要求 model 先輸出 🟠 收到/打算（session 首次）+ ⚠️ 因果鏈分析，重試即放行。\\n * 誠實邊界：只強制「擋一次逼輸出」，分析深度仍是文字層自律。\\n *\\n * 範圍：只閘專案內檔案；.claude/ 內與專案外路徑直接放行。\\n * 狀態：os.tmpdir()/impact-gate/<session_id>/，session 結束自然作廢。\\n * 失敗策略：任何解析錯誤 fail-open（exit 0），不擋正常工作。\\n */\\nimport { createHash } from \\\"node:crypto\\\";\\nimport { existsSync, mkdirSync, writeFileSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport { join, resolve } from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { tmpdir } from \\\"node:os\\\";\\n\\nconst chunks = [];\\nprocess.stdin.on(\\\"data\\\", (c) => chunks.push(c));\\nprocess.stdin.on(\\\"end\\\", () => {\\n  try {\\n    main(JSON.parse(Buffer.concat(chunks).toString(\\\"utf8\\\")));\\n  } catch {\\n    process.exit(0); // fail-open\\n  }\\n});\\n\\nfunction main(input) {\\n  const filePath =\\n    input?.tool_input?.file_path ?? input?.tool_input?.notebook_path ?? \\\"\\\";\\n  const sessionId = String(input?.session_id ?? \\\"unknown\\\").replace(/[^\\\\w-]/g, \\\"_\\\");\\n  if (!filePath) process.exit(0);\\n\\n  const norm = resolve(filePath).replace(/\\\\\\\\/g, \\\"/\\\").toLowerCase();\\n  const root = resolve(process.cwd()).replace(/\\\\\\\\/g, \\\"/\\\").toLowerCase() + \\\"/\\\";\\n\\n  // 只閘專案內檔案；.claude/（設定、hook 自身、todo 等）不閘\\n  if (!norm.startsWith(root) || norm.includes(\\\"/.claude/\\\")) process.exit(0);\\n\\n  const stateDir = join(tmpdir(), \\\"impact-gate\\\", sessionId);\\n  mkdirSync(stateDir, { recursive: true });\\n\\n  const sessionMarker = join(stateDir, \\\"_session\\\");\\n  const fileMarker = join(stateDir, createHash(\\\"sha1\\\").update(norm).digest(\\\"hex\\\"));\\n\\n  if (existsSync(fileMarker)) process.exit(0); // 此檔已放行過\\n\\n  const firstInSession = !existsSync(sessionMarker);\\n  // 先蓋章再阻擋：下次重試同一修改即放行\\n  writeFileSync(sessionMarker, \\\"\\\");\\n  writeFileSync(fileMarker, \\\"\\\");\\n\\n  const lines = [\\n    `[impact-gate] 首次修改 ${filePath} — 先輸出以下分析，然後重試同一修改（重試即放行）：`,\\n  ];\\n  if (firstInSession) {\\n    lines.push(\\\"🟠 收到：[用戶意圖]\\\", \\\"🟠 打算：[一句話]\\\");\\n  }\\n  lines.push(\\n    \\\"⚠️ [因果鏈分析] 修改 {檔案}:{函式}\\\",\\n    \\\"├─ 根因：為什麼要改\\\",\\n    \\\"├─ 呼叫者：grep N 個逐一分析（檔:行 — 影響 — 需連動是/否）；呼叫者=0 → 停，先找真正執行路徑\\\",\\n    \\\"├─ 隱性風險：快取失效/時序變化/語意漂移\\\",\\n    \\\"└─ 決策：連動清單或不需要的理由\\\",\\n  );\\n  process.stderr.write(lines.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\"));\\n  process.exit(2);\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":69,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":69}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

input?.tool_input?.notebook_path ?? \\\"\\\";\\n  const sessionId = String(input?.session_id ?? \\\"unknown\\\").replace(/[^\\\\w-]/g, \\\"_\\\");\\n  if (!filePath) process.exit(0);\\n\\n  const norm = resolve(filePath).replace(/\\\\\\\\/g, \\\"/\\\").toLowerCase();\\n  const root = resolve(process.cwd()).replace(/\\\\\\\\/g, \\\"/\\\").toLowerCase() + \\\"/\\\";\\n\\n  // 只閘專案內檔案；.claude/（設定、hook 自身、todo 等）不閘\\n  if (!norm.startsWith(root) || norm.includes(\\\"/.claude/\\\")) process.exit(0);\\n\\n  const stateDir = join(tmpdir(), \\\"impact-gate\\\", sessionId);\\n  mkdirSync(stateDir, { recursive: true });\\n\\n  const sessionMarker = join(stateDir, \\\"_session\\\");\\n  const fileMarker = join(stateDir, createHash(\\\"sha1\\\").update(norm).digest(\\\"hex\\\"));\\n\\n  if (existsSync(fileMarker)) process.exit(0); // 此檔已放行過\\n\\n  const firstInSession = !existsSync(sessionMarker);\\n  // 先蓋章再阻擋：下次重試同一修改即放行\\n  writeFileSync(sessionMarker, \\\"\\\");\\n  writeFileSync(fileMarker, \\\"\\\");\\n\\n  const lines = [\\n    `[impact-gate] 首次修改 ${filePath} — 先輸出以下分析，然後重試同一修改（重試即放行）：`,\\n  ];\\n  if (firstInSession) {\\n    lines.push(\\\"🟠 收到：[用戶意圖]\\\", \\\"🟠 打算：[一句話]\\\");\\n  }\\n  lines.push(\\n    \\\"⚠️ [因果鏈分析] 修改 {檔案}:{函式}\\\",\\n    \\\"├─ 根因：為什麼要改\\\",\\n    \\\"├─ 呼叫者：grep N 個逐一分析（檔:行 — 影響 — 需連動是/否）；呼叫者=0 → 停，先找真正執行路徑\\\",\\n    \\\"├─ 隱性風險：快取失效/時序變化/語意漂移\\\",\\n    \\\"└─ 決策：連動清單或不需要的理由\\\",\\n  );\\n  process.stderr.write(lines.join(\\\"\\\\n\\\"));\\n  process.exit(2);\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":69,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":69}}"</outcome>

content.thinking:
The user is now reading the impact-gate.mjs hook file. This is a PreToolUse hook that gates Edit/Write operations to force the model to output impact analysis before making changes.

Let me analyze what was learned:
1. This is a PreToolUse hook that intercepts Edit/Write operations
2. It blocks the first Edit/Write attempt per file per session (exit 2)
3. Forces the model to output:
   - 🟠 收到/打算 (session first time only)
   - ⚠️ 因果鏈分析 (causal chain analysis)
4. After outputting the analysis, retry is allowed (markers are written before blocking)
5. Only gates project files, excludes .claude/ directory
6. State stored in os.tmpdir()/impact-gate/<session_id>/
7. Fail-open strategy on parsing errors

This is a discovery about how the bridge's impact gating mechanism works.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Impact-gate hook enforces causal chain analysis before file modifications</title>
  <subtitle>PreToolUse hook blocks first Edit/Write per file, requiring impact analysis output before retry succeeds</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>impact-gate.mjs is a PreToolUse hook that intercepts Edit and Write tool calls</fact>
    <fact>First Edit/Write attempt per file per session exits with code 2, forcing model to output analysis</fact>
    <fact>Session and file markers written to os.tmpdir()/impact-gate/<session_id>/ before blocking, so retry immediately succeeds</fact>
    <fact>Hook only gates project files; .claude/ directory and paths outside project root bypass the gate</fact>
    <fact>Required output includes 🟠 收到/打算 (session first time) and ⚠️ 因果鏈分析 with root cause, callers, hidden risks, and decision</fact>
    <fact>Fail-open strategy: any parsing error exits 0 to avoid blocking normal work</fact>
    <fact>Causal chain analysis must include grep of N callers, analyze each for impact and whether cascade changes needed</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The bridge implements a PreToolUse hook called impact-gate that enforces disciplined change analysis. When Claude attempts to Edit or Write a project file for the first time in a session, the hook blocks the operation (exit 2) and requires the model to first output a structured impact analysis. On the session's very first modification, Claude must state what it received from the user (🟠 收到) and what it intends to do (🟠 打算). For every first-time file modification, it must output a causal chain analysis (⚠️ 因果鏈分析) covering: the root cause for the change, grep results of callers with individual impact analysis, hidden risks like cache invalidation or semantic drift, and a final decision on what cascade changes are needed. The hook writes state markers before blocking, so the immediate retry of the same modification succeeds without re-analysis. This creates a "gate A/B" pattern: attempt → blocked with analysis requirement → output analysis → retry → allowed. Files in .claude/ and paths outside the project root bypass the gate entirely. Any parsing errors fail-open (exit 0) to prevent the hook from interfering with normal work.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>why-it-exists</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>.claude/hooks/impact-gate.mjs</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
